reasons: Minsitthar directly attacks Joy's repeated dash identity.
King's Calling prevents movement skills inside the field, making Joy's rhythm chain much harder to execute.

proof.summary: Joy wants to chain dashes through enemies or crystals, while Minsitthar's ultimate creates a field where enemies cannot use movement skills.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar casts after Joy commits into the fight.
Allies are ready to punish Joy inside the field.
failureCases: Joy baits King's Calling then re-enters later.
Minsitthar misses the field or catches only low-value targets.

reasons: Khufra's Bouncing Ball punishes Joy when she tries to dash through him.
He can protect backliners from Joy's entry path.

proof.summary: Joy depends on repeated dash movement, while Khufra can knock up enemies that attempt to blink across him.
worksBestWhen: Khufra stands between Joy and the carry.
Khufra activates Bouncing Ball during Joy's dash chain.
failureCases: Joy routes around Khufra instead of through him.

reasons: Kaja can suppress Joy during her commit window and prevent her from freely finishing the dash and ultimate sequence.
Dragging Joy away from her intended path breaks her fight rhythm.

proof.summary: Joy wants continuous movement and timing, while Kaja's suppression stops her and gives the team a clear punish window.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits until Joy has committed forward.
failureCases: Joy keeps distance until Kaja's ultimate is used.
Kaja is unable to reach Joy before she completes her damage window.

reasons: Franco's suppression can stop Joy even when normal crowd-control timing is hard.
Hook threat also limits Joy's flank routes before teamfights.

proof.summary: Joy is difficult to control while moving, but Franco can suppress her when she commits or catch her before she starts her rhythm chain.
worksBestWhen: Franco holds Bloody Hunt for Joy's entry.
The team has burst ready during suppression.
failureCases: Joy avoids Franco's range and attacks another angle.
Franco misses hook or suppresses a less important hero.

reasons: Obsidia can directly punish Joy's dash-based rhythm if Hunt of Bone hits, because Joy depends on repeated movement skills to build her damage and ultimate window.
Compared with Esmeralda, Obsidia has a clearer hard-counter interaction, while Esmeralda is more of a sustain and shield skirmish answer.

proof.summary: Joy wants to chain repeated dashes to build her rhythm and unlock her ultimate, while Obsidia's Hunt of Bone can tether Joy and prevent displacement skills if it lands.
worksBestWhen: Obsidia casts Hunt of Bone after Joy commits forward.
Joy has not yet completed her full rhythm chain.
Obsidia has teammate follow-up or enough damage to punish during the tether.
failureCases: Joy baits the ultimate, then re-enters after cooldown.
Joy completes her rhythm and damage window before Obsidia can lock her.
